positive_reviews: Selsun Blue Dandruff Control Shampoo with Conditioner has clearly made a significant impact on managing your dandruff concerns, a testament to its effectiveness for those with persistent scalp issues. Your experience suggests that even without a formal diagnosis, possibly of eczema, this medicated shampoo has provided reliable relief where other brands have been part of your regimen, such as Head and Shoulders. The recommendation to use just a dime-sized amount daily underlines the product's efficiency and helps justify the cost, despite its smaller size compared to other options. Your approach to hair washing, balancing the need to manage oiliness with the advice against daily shampooing, is a common dilemma. Particularly for those with longer hair that tends to get oily, the temptation to wash more frequently is understandable. It's noteworthy that Selsun Blue not only addresses dandruff but also contributes to your hair feeling beautiful, dry, and voluminous. This dual benefit of controlling dandruff while enhancing hair's aesthetic qualities, even in the face of challenges like oiliness and styling habits, highlights Selsun Blue's value in your hair care routine. Your practical insights into managing dandruff with Selsun Blue, alongside other brands for variety, offer a helpful perspective for anyone struggling with similar issues. It serves as a reminder that finding the right hair care product can involve trial, personalization, and sometimes, balancing effectiveness with frequency of use and cost.

I shave the sides and back of my head daily. Due to this I sometimes get razor burns or just straight up acne spots. I have also off and on fought acne as my entire adult life usually if Iâm going through a lot of stress. Thatâs what I attribute it to anyway. So anyway, when Iâm showering I use this shampoo daily as a head and face wash. Then I use Oxy Facewash with benzoyl peroxide. I leave the oxy on for a minute or so then shave, utilizing the oxy as my shaving cream. I follow up with isopropyl alcohol on the shaved parts of my head with a cotton ball. It may sound extreme but it really doesnât take that much longer when I shower in the morning and more importantly, it works, at least for me. In fact, this combo worked when nothing else really did.

I have been suffering from extreme scalp scum (no better way to describe it) on the back of my head. I would literally scrape and come away with all this yellowish matter under my nails. I wondered how my hair could even grow through it. I peeled off big hunks of dandruff all day long. I was to the point where I needed to take serious action. I went to my dermatologist, asking for some solution. I figured I was way beyond an over-the-counter product since H&S and tar shampoos made no difference at all. Instead of prescribing something, he recommended Selsun Blue. I thought he was kidding, but he wasn't. So I bought a bottle and honestly, I cannot believe it worked! But it worked!!! From my very first wash, everything changed! I'm sort of in disbelief, and bummed that I didn't try this product sooner. Highly recommend!

negative_reviews: They made a new formula thatâs supposed to be cleaner. Iâm not liking it as much as the old one. I havenât checked the ingredients to see what was left out. I primarily use it to wash my shaved head and beard. Itâs always done a great job at decreasing razor burns, bumps, pimples, and dandruff. However, the new formula barely lathers up and feels like I have to use twice as much. It also isnât even close to the same pleasant scent as before. I may be switching brands after this bottle, but Iâll keep using it to see if my opinion changes. *update* After reading each bottle, it makes sense why it has no lather and barely any scent. They removed all the natural plant oils that were in the first. It seems like more than anything, they were trying to save money. I understand how some of the removed ingredients , including those for lather, could be irritating, but the new formula seems like it was much more likely to have been devised by the finance team. Here is what was taken out and whatâs new: Ingredients Removed in the New Formula: PEG-11 Sunflower Glycerides Cocamidopropyl Betaine Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Flower Extract Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Extract Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Extract Acacia Farnesiana Flower Extract Tocopheryl Acetate Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice Panthenol Cocamide MEA Sodium Lauryl Glucose Carboxylate Benzyl Alcohol Lauryl Glucoside Polyquaternium-57 Stearamidopropyl PG-Dimonium Chloride Phosphate Potassium Hydroxide Disodium EDTA Benzophenone-4 Isopropyl Myristate Propylene Glycol New Ingredients in the New Formula: Dihydrogenated Tallow Phthalic Acid Amide Cocopolyol Betaine Benzoic Acid Titanium Dioxide Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose Magnesium Aluminum Silicate Sodium Chloride Sodium Isethionate DMDM Hydantoin Blue 1 *update 12-11-24* I will not be purchasing again. It seems to be more irritating than the original formula.

I've been using this Shampoo for years. I was surprised to find out when my last order arrived that the bottle had changed and the label indicated "New Cleaner Formula." It is indeed a new formula, and this is what has changed: - It doesn't lather up as well - it doesn't leave my hair feeling as clean - it smells like toilet cleaner I did some brief research online and see that the company that makes this product is trying to spin-off it's consumer healthcare line, which I assume this falls under. So unfortunately this product is the latest victim of shareholder value that inevitably is making life worse for everyone else but shareholders. My advice, if you used this product and liked it, try to find the old version and buy as much as you can. Unfortunately my efforts to do so have not been successful.

When I had trouble finding this product in my local grocery stores, I purchased is through Amazon. The bottle came as expected...the ubiquitous blue color...and I expected the shampoo to have the same, deep blue color. It's been like this for decades. But the color of this "new" version is peachy-cream, and it was a disappointment. But, we could live with this, thinking that maybe the blue dye is not good for the planet. What we find obnoxious, however, is the odor of this product. SULPHUR foulness permeates the shower while shampooing, and it sticks to everything. PLUS, there's no pleasant tingling sensation. Both the smell and tingling came from the menthol infused in this product. We'll be looking for a similar product from a different company from now on.

positive_reviews: Review: Selsun Blue Medicated Antidandruff Shampoo I've been struggling with dandruff for years and have tried countless products with mixed results. After hearing recommendations from friends, I decided to give Selsun Blue Medicated Antidandruff Shampoo a try, and I'm genuinely impressed with the results. First, the effectiveness cannot be overstated. Within just a week of regular use, I noticed a significant reduction in flakes and scalp irritation. By the second week, my dandruff was virtually gone. The shampoo lathers well and leaves my hair feeling clean without stripping away natural oils or causing dryness. The formula seems to work exceptionally well on my sensitive scalp, providing long-lasting relief between washes. I've found that using it 2-3 times per week maintains the results perfectly, and I can alternate with my regular shampoo on other days. One thing to note is the scent - it's quite strong and medicinal. While this is understandable given its therapeutic nature, the powerful smell lingers even after rinsing thoroughly. For those sensitive to fragrances, this might be something to consider. I personally find that using a nicely scented conditioner afterward helps balance it out. The bottle design makes it easy to dispense the right amount, and a little goes a long way, making it good value despite the slightly premium price point. Overall, I'd highly recommend Selsun Blue for anyone dealing with persistent dandruff or scalp issues. Just be prepared for the strong scent - it's a small trade-off for how well it works!

I've been using this shampoo since June 2024. It was a godsend helping me with my severe scalp issues. My scalp was heavily inflamed with soreness, redness, bumps, scabs, flakes, pus, bleeding. Horrible. After the first few days of using this there was a dramatic improvement in my scalp. I then started using a sulfur hair grease along with this shampoo and my scalp became 100% clear. They switched the formula around I think my second or third bottle and I was worried it wouldn't work, but it was still good. I have curly afro hair and wash my hair virtually everyday with this shampoo and it doesn't dry out my hair or scalp. This may be the shampoo for you if you struggle with scalp issues and shampoo like Head and Shoulders doesn't work for you. That was me, Head and Shoulders did nothing. Full disclosure, while I do love this shampoo, I am trying out a different shampoo with sulfur in it. I noticed during the week I went without my sulfur grease, but still using this shampoo, a couple of scabs formed on my scalp. So I want to try something different. If I don't like the new shampoo, I'll definitely come back to this one.

negative_reviews: When I had trouble finding this product in my local grocery stores, I purchased is through Amazon. The bottle came as expected...the ubiquitous blue color...and I expected the shampoo to have the same, deep blue color. It's been like this for decades. But the color of this "new" version is peachy-cream, and it was a disappointment. But, we could live with this, thinking that maybe the blue dye is not good for the planet. What we find obnoxious, however, is the odor of this product. SULPHUR foulness permeates the shower while shampooing, and it sticks to everything. PLUS, there's no pleasant tingling sensation. Both the smell and tingling came from the menthol infused in this product. We'll be looking for a similar product from a different company from now on.

Selsun has completely redesigned this product and it is basically something all-new in the old packaging. The new version is green, not tan and stinks horribly. It is also terrible at cleaning your hair leaves it feeling as it had never been washed at all. If it could be retuned I would have done so but sadly this product is not eligible for returns. I tried calling Selsun customer support but it has been moved overseas and of course they can't do anything. I highly recommend buying the regular Selsun Blue or considering other products instead.
